title: File-Delivery Outcomes and Typed Evidence · Inference Brief: Custody Effect Boundary
summary: Predict unknown input, typed state, command, stream, promise, storage, cache, security, cost, and recovery outcomes before reviewing typed file-delivery outcome dossier; use discriminated result unions and exhaustive changed-outcome evaluation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the custody effect boundary increment in typed file-delivery outcome dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, trace browser, Node service, object store, CDN, identity provider, scanner, database, and stakeholder custody without assigning evidence a boundary cannot observe.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, define file-delivery success with learner, uploader, viewer, owner, security, accessibility, operations, and finance evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Custody Effect Boundary · Theory Custody Effect Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 8191 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to evaluate a discriminated stakeholder outcome from authorization, custody, accessibility, and cost; retain keyboard and screen-reader progress, cancellation, error, and recovery evidence; then change object version, signed header, or cache dimension and reject stale authority. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Custody Effect Boundary model · Theory Custody Effect Bou…, keep Custody Effect Boundary connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, trace browser, Node service, object store, CDN, identity provider, scanner, database, and stakeholder custody without assigning evidence a boundary cannot observe.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Custody Effect Boundary model · Theory Custody Effect Bou…, name the stakeholder, unknown schema, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, promise, stream, cache, cost, abort, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Custody Effect Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Custody Effect Boundary model · Theory Custody Effect Bou…, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 4953 during a regional recovery and budget exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to evaluate a discriminated stakeholder outcome from authorization, custody, accessibility, and cost; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, trace browser, Node service, object store, CDN, identity provider, scanner, database, and stakeholder custody without assigning evidence a boundary cannot observe.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Custody Effect Boundary model · Theory Custody Effect Bou…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Once a request body reaches the Node handler, the file is durably stored and usable. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.
